pattern: Every match eliminates exactly one team, so a single-elimination bracket down to one champion always plays exactly (teams - 1) matches regardless of how byes are handled.
hints: Think about what a single match actually does to the pool of teams still in the running: it removes exactly one loser.
The tournament keeps going, round after round (with byes for odd counts), until only one team remains.
Since every match removes exactly one team and the tournament ends with exactly one team left, the total number of matches is simply the starting team count minus one.

edge_inputs.description: an odd number of teams needing byes
a single team, no matches at all
a power of two minus one, stressing the odd-bye path repeatedly

stress.description: every possible team count across the constraint range
